mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in FEDFUNDS raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as the rate trend continues.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend slope and forward 20d returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or returns are positive in more than 55% of periods with rising FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation crosses above a high threshold, markets price in accelerated Fed tightening, causing a repricing shock that depresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns following CPI YoY breaches above 6% are non-negative on average across the 2021-2022 sample, or Rank IC < 0.03 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rising FEDFUNDS and falling UNRATE indicate an overheating labor market that the Fed must aggressively counter, jointly signaling a higher terminal rate path than either series implies alone, which depresses equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rising 3-month trend and UNRATE falling 3-month trend simultaneously
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with tight labor market
failure_condition: The joint-trigger sample shows forward 60d SPX returns not significantly different from the single-factor FEDFUNDS-only baseline (p > 0.05), indicating no incremental interaction effect.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns only when FEDFUNDS is simultaneously rising, because housing weakness under tightening conditions signals broader demand destruction; the same HOUST decline during easing regimes does not carry the same negative signal and may even coincide with positive returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 5% over 3 months
failure_condition: HOUST declines during tightening regimes are followed by positive or neutral forward 20d returns in more than 50% of instances, or the direction of the HOUST-return relationship does not flip when tested separately in an easing-regime subsample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in INDPRO reflects expanding industrial output and corporate earnings capacity, which supports positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as long as the production expansion trend persists.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO trailing growth and forward 60d returns falls below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or returns are negative in over half of periods with positive INDPRO momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in RSAFS signals weakening consumer spending, a leading indicator of slowing aggregate demand and corporate revenue growth, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month once retail sales growth turns negative.
failure_condition: Forward 20d SPX returns following a negative RSAFS MoM print are positive on average across the sample, or the signal's hit rate falls below 50% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in PAYEMS 3-month average job gains below a critical pace signals an inflection toward labor market softening ahead of broader macro data, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the near-term one-week horizon as markets rapidly reprice growth expectations.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average net change falls below 150,000
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns after PAYEMS deceleration below the threshold are positive in more than 55% of instances, or Rank IC stays below 0.02 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Slowing GDPC1 growth combined with persistently elevated CPIAUCSL jointly signal a stagflationary environment in which the Fed cannot ease to support growth, causing sustained equity de-rating and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon that a single indicator alone would not fully capture.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth below 1.5% while CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 6%
failure_condition: Forward 60d SPX returns in the joint low-growth/high-inflation trigger sample are not significantly more negative than the unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), indicating no meaningful interaction effect beyond either series alone.
